Reading covers exactly the topic headline: API concept, client-server model, request components (endpoint/method/headers/body), response components (status code/headers/body), JSON as format (not parsing), why APIs exist.
JSON body and response examples are illustrative of what a request/response looks like — not teaching parsing. Parsing explicitly deferred to 12.12.

Authentication topic introduced only at the level of 'API key in headers' — setup details deferred to 12.10, consistent with scope.

All acronyms defined on first use: API (Application Programming Interface, line 5 + §Key Concepts expanded word-by-word), LLM (Large Language Model, line 5), HTTP (HyperText Transfer Protocol, line 28), URL (Uniform Resource Locator, line 60), JSON (JavaScript Object Notation, line 80).
No paragraph introduces 3+ new terms simultaneously.

Concrete-first throughout: wall-socket analogy before abstract API definition; restaurant/waiter analogy before client-server abstraction; worked example grounds the full cycle in a single concrete scenario.
Sentence lengths average well under 25 words. No hedging phrases detected ('it is generally considered', 'many experts believe', etc.). Active voice throughout.
No assessment references (A4 due week 13 not mentioned anywhere in the reading).
No paragraph exceeds 6 sentences — most are 2-4 sentences.
Sequences and multi-item lists rendered as numbered lists (6-step worked example, 3-step HTTP cycle) and bullet lists (In Practice do/avoid lists, Key Takeaways).
Tables used appropriately for client-server roles, waiter analogy, HTTP methods, and status codes — not prose run-ons.
Bold term / plain-meaning pattern used consistently throughout Key Concepts (e.g., '**Endpoint** is the address...', '**Headers** are metadata...').
Inline examples present in Key Concepts (endpoint URL example, status code table, JSON body snippet) — not confined to Worked Example section alone.

verdict: PASS — all 8 checks pass. Format discipline: clean H1 opener, no frontmatter, sidecar valid, six fixed sections in correct order. Length: 1441 words / 6.3 minutes — within [1200, 1600] / [6, 8]. Citations: primary blog.postman.com [1] cited 7 times; [2] and [3] both used with matching bibliography; no orphan markers. Forward references: no json module, no SDK calls, no content[0] extraction — all downstream topics (12.10, 12.12) are clean named-and-deferred pointers only. Scope discipline: reading stays on API concept, request/response anatomy, and JSON as a format — no parsing, no SDK constructor, no authentication setup beyond 'API key goes in headers'. Beginner readability: all acronyms spelled out on first use, concrete-before-abstract (analogies lead), no jargon dumps, no assessment references. Formatting: tables, numbered lists, and bullet lists throughout — no walls of text. Faithfulness: all 10 sampled assertions confirmed in corpus. Diagram embedded in Key Concepts section as required by artifact_plan.
